structured_content.text:
View
                        email in browser Hearst Downsizing, Ben Shapiro’s Payday, Christie’s Choreography Welcome back to The Daily Courant, your afternoon dispatch featuring Puck’s best new reporting. Today, we lead with Marion Maneker ’s exclusive look at the behind-the-scenes choreography at Christie’s that allowed a small gouache work on paper from René Magritte to draft on the $121 million sale of a masterwork by the Belgian surrealist and set a bombshell record of its own. Plus, below the fold: Abby Livingston anticipates the headwinds facing one of Elon Musk ’s initial DOGE targets—the Pentagon’s $2 trillion F-35 Joint Strike Fighter program—and how it puts him at odds with Trump . On The Grill Room , Dylan Byers and The Daily Wire’s Jeremy Boreing explore the surging impact of alternative media on modern politics and the strategies
driving his company’s accelerated growth. On The Varsity , John Ourand and analyst extraordinaire Michael Nathanson predict how sports leagues and college conferences can thrive in the post-TV era. And on The Powers That Be , Jon Kelly reunites with Peter Hamby to make sense of Hearst’s brutal pre-Thanksgiving massacre and forecast what covering Trump II will entail for media companies and newsrooms alike. The Making of an $18.8 Million Mini Magritte MARION MANEKER The behind-the-scenes story of how a small gouache work from the Belgian master leveraged timing, demand, and deal heat to draft off the Ertegun piece for a new record of its own. A MESSAGE FROM Instagram Instagram Teen Accounts: a protected experience for teens, guided by parents Instagram Teen Accounts are designed to address parents’ biggest concerns, providing automatic protections for who can contact their teens and the content they can see. The impact: Built-in limits give parents more peace of mind when it comes to protecting their teens. Learn more DOGE-Ball: Elon vs. The F-35 ABBY LIVINGSTON The Pentagon’s most expensive program has a powerful new enemy in Elon Musk. But the electric-car, social-media, and rocket tycoon faces two major obstacles in his quest for efficiency: Congress, and his buddy in the White House. New Right Media’s Billion-Dollar Play DYLAN BYERS Jeremy Boreing, co-founder of the conservative media network The Daily Wire, joins Dylan to explore the surging impact of alternative media on modern politics and deliver his candid critique of legacy media's failures. Then he shares insights into the strategies driving his company’s accelerated growth and discusses what lies ahead as The Daily Wire closes in on surpassing $200 million in revenue. The Live Sports Streaming Playbook JOHN OURAND Michael Nathanson, the co-founder and top analyst at MoffettNathanson, joins John for a rollicking exploration of the challenges facing legacy media now that streaming is king. Then they dive into how sports leagues and college conferences can survive, and thrive, in the post-TV era. Media Monday: D.N.C. Campaigns, Hearst Downsizing & More PETER HAMBY & JON KELLY Jon Kelly rejoins Peter for a spirited dissection of the recent Hearst mega-layoffs, the media-heavy politicking around the D.N.C. chairmanship, and the impact of Trumponomics on the industry. Need help? Review our FAQs
                                    page or contact
                                    us for assistance. For brand partnerships, email ads@puck.news . You received this email because you signed up to receive emails from Puck, or as part of your Puck account associated with puck@khamel.com. To stop receiving this newsletter and/or manage all your email preferences, click here . Puck is published by Heat Media LLC. 227 W 17th St New York, NY 10011.
